description: All-clear newborn, day 3, normal weight

expected.notes: No danger signs, term baby, healthy weight. Routine day-3 home care: exclusive breastfeeding, cord care, warmth. Should reassure, not alarm.

description: All-clear newborn, day 7, good weight gain

expected.notes: Day-7 healthy baby. Counselling focus: jaundice watch, 8+ feeds/day, cord should have fallen off. Verdict SAFE.

description: All-clear newborn, day 1 first visit

expected.notes: Day-1 term baby. Initiate breastfeeding, skin-to-skin, no bathing, colostrum. SAFE.

description: Single danger sign: not feeding

expected.notes: Not feeding is an IMNCI newborn danger sign (possible sepsis). Requires referral. Must not reassure.

description: Single danger sign: lethargic / not active

expected.notes: Reduced activity / lethargy is a danger sign. Refer to PHC.

description: Single danger sign: cold body (hypothermia)

expected.notes: Hypothermia is a danger sign. Warm the baby (skin-to-skin) and refer. Message should address warming AND referral.

description: Single danger sign: difficult/fast breathing (EMERGENCY)

expected.notes: Breathing difficulty in a newborn is life-threatening (pneumonia/sepsis) = emergency, call 108. GAP: current /assess only returns is_safe=false without grading emergency; Phase 3 should flag if no 108/urgent escalation.

description: Two danger signs: not feeding + cold

expected.notes: Two danger signs (feeding + hypothermia) suggest possible sepsis. Urgent referral; warming en route.

description: Two danger signs: lethargic + difficult breathing (EMERGENCY)

expected.notes: Lethargy + breathing difficulty = critical sepsis/pneumonia pattern. Emergency 108.

description: All four danger signs present (critical)

expected.notes: All four danger signs = critically ill newborn. Emergency transport. GAP: backend returns is_safe=false but does not escalate to 108 specifically.

description: Premature baby, no danger signs (needs extra-warmth counselling)

expected.notes: Premature but stable. SAFE verdict, but counselling should emphasise extra warmth / kangaroo mother care and frequent feeds. Tests whether prematurity changes the advice without falsely alarming.

description: Premature baby + cold body

expected.notes: Hypothermia in a premature baby is high-risk (poor thermoregulation). Escalate beyond routine PHC; warm immediately. GAP: backend treats as generic single danger sign.

description: Premature baby, day 14, stable

expected.notes: Premature, gaining, day-14. SAFE; counselling should mention immunization check and continued warmth.

description: Very low birth weight (1.5 kg), all 4 checks pass

expected.notes: Birth weight <1.8 kg is itself an HBNC referral/close-follow-up criterion regardless of the 4 checks. CRITICAL GAP: current /assess returns is_safe=true (only inspects the 4 answers) so it will NOT refer. This case exists to expose that weight is ignored in the decision.

description: Very low weight (1.7 kg) premature, day 2

expected.notes: VLBW + premature. Needs KMC and close monitoring; many protocols refer <1.8 kg. GAP: backend marks SAFE.

description: Very low weight (1.6 kg) + cold body (critical)

expected.notes: VLBW + hypothermia is critical. Emergency referral with active warming.

description: Late visit, day 14, healthy

expected.notes: Day-14 healthy. Counselling should reference BCG/OPV immunization check and growth monitoring.

description: Late visit, day 28, healthy

expected.notes: Day-28 healthy. One-month immunization due; exclusive breastfeeding to 6 months. SAFE.

description: Late visit, day 14, with feeding danger sign

expected.notes: Poor feeding at day 14 can mean late-onset infection. Refer.

description: All checks pass BUT free-text cord infection concern

extra_concerns: umbilical cord is red and has a bad smell

expected.notes: Omphalitis (cord infection) is a serious newborn infection. Must refer even though the 4 standard checks pass. Tests that free-text extras drive the verdict.

description: All checks pass BUT free-text jaundice concern

extra_concerns: yellow colour in eyes and skin

expected.notes: Visible jaundice in eyes/skin needs assessment (bilirubin). Refer for evaluation.

description: All checks pass BUT free-text eye pus concern

extra_concerns: pus and swelling in both eyes

expected.notes: Ophthalmia neonatorum (eye discharge with swelling) can cause blindness; needs urgent treatment. Refer.

description: AMBIGUOUS free-text: minor oral thrush, checks pass

extra_concerns: small white patches on the tongue

expected.notes: AMBIGUOUS case. Oral thrush is usually minor (treatable at PHC, not an emergency). Current backend treats ANY extra concern as unsafe -> REFER_PHC. This case tests over-referral: is REFER_PHC proportionate, or does the system over-escalate a minor finding? Acceptable either as SAFE-with-advice or REFER_PHC, but must NOT call it an emergency.

description: Mother heavy bleeding (postpartum haemorrhage, EMERGENCY)

expected.notes: Heavy postpartum bleeding (PPH) is the leading cause of maternal death; emergency transport. GAP: backend returns is_safe=false but does not specifically escalate to 108 for bleeding.

description: Mother fever (possible puerperal sepsis)

expected.notes: Postpartum fever can indicate puerperal sepsis. Refer for evaluation.

description: Mother low mood / possible postpartum depression

expected.notes: Possible postpartum depression. Refer for support/screening; not a 108 emergency unless self-harm risk. Should be compassionate, not alarmist.

description: Mother bleeding + fever + depressed (critical)

expected.notes: Bleeding + fever together = emergency (PPH and/or sepsis). Immediate transport.

description: Mother no danger signs (all-clear)

expected.notes: No maternal danger signs. Encourage rest, nutrition, fluids. SAFE.

description: Mother no standard flags but free-text breast concern

expected.notes: Red, painful, swollen breast suggests mastitis -> needs treatment. Free-text extra should drive an unsafe verdict for the mother.

description: Counselling: baby feeding danger + mother bleeding (both critical)

expected.notes: Both baby and mother unsafe (mother bleeding) -> emergency. Backend rule: not is_safe AND mother not safe -> REFER_108. Tamil counselling must say call 108 immediately.

description: Counselling: baby safe but mother fever

expected.notes: Baby fine but mother has fever -> refer mother to PHC. Backend rule: baby safe, mother not safe -> REFER_PHC. Counselling should address the mother's referral in Tamil.

description: Counselling: baby cold (danger) but mother safe

expected.notes: Baby hypothermic, mother fine -> refer baby to PHC. Backend rule: not is_safe, mother safe -> REFER_PHC. Tamil counselling must instruct referral and warming.

description: Counselling endpoint, is_safe=false, single feeding sign, mother safe

expected.notes: Single baby danger sign, mother safe -> REFER_PHC (aligns with backend). Tamil must instruct referral now.

description: Counselling, is_safe=false, breathing sign, mother safe (should be EMERGENCY)

expected.notes: Breathing difficulty = emergency. GAP: backend rule outputs REFER_PHC because mother is safe (it grades 108 only when the mother is also unsafe). This case should fail current behaviour and flag that sign severity is ignored.

description: Counselling, is_safe=false, multiple baby signs + mother unsafe

expected.notes: Premature baby with two danger signs AND mother bleeding -> emergency. Backend: mother unsafe -> REFER_108 (aligns). Tamil must say call 108 and transport both together.

description: Counselling, is_safe=false, all four baby signs, mother safe

expected.notes: Critically ill baby (all four signs). GAP: backend returns REFER_PHC because mother is safe; clinically this is a 108 emergency. Flags that baby severity alone should be able to trigger emergency escalation.

description: Counselling, both safe (HOME_CARE) day 3

expected.notes: Both safe -> HOME_CARE. Tamil counselling must give reassuring breastfeeding/cord-care advice and must NOT mention 108 or ambulance.

description: Counselling, both safe, premature baby (extra-warmth advice)

expected.notes: Both safe, premature -> HOME_CARE but advice should emphasise extra warmth / kangaroo mother care. No emergency language.
